preamble: The transformation from an alveolar flap to a voiceless retroflex stop is a process of fortition and retroflexion, typically occurring when a rhotic merges with a following dental or alveolar consonant.

examples.note: In various dialects, the alveolar flap triggers retroflexion in following alveolar stops, merging into a single retroflex stop.
Exhibits variation between voiceless rhotics/flaps and voiceless retroflex stops.

sources: Kristoffersen, G. (2000). The Phonology of Norwegian.
Sagna, S. (2008). A Philosophical and Linguistic Analysis of the Gújjolaay Eegimaa (Bayot) Language.
